meta.title: Cholesterol Ratio Calculator — Total/HDL, LDL/HDL & Triglyceride/HDL | Health Calculators
meta.description: Calculate cholesterol ratios (Total/HDL, LDL/HDL, Triglyceride/HDL) and assess cardiovascular risk per AHA/ESC 2023. Supports Friedewald formula and mmol/L conversion.

cholesterolRatio.title: Cholesterol Ratio Calculator
cholesterolRatio.description: Calculate cholesterol ratios and assess cardiovascular risk per AHA/ESC 2023 guidelines.
cholesterolRatio.ldlHint: Leave empty for Friedewald estimate (only valid when triglycerides < 400 mg/dL)
cholesterolRatio.warningTrigHighForLdl: Triglycerides ≥ 400 mg/dL: Friedewald LDL estimate is not valid. LDL/HDL ratio is omitted.
cholesterolRatio.warningPlausibility: Input values are outside the clinically plausible range. Please check your values.

hint.optimal: Favorable lipid profile. Continue with a heart-healthy diet and regular physical activity.
hint.moderate: Moderately elevated risk. Lifestyle changes (diet, exercise) are recommended. Consider consulting a physician.
hint.high: Elevated cardiovascular risk. Medical evaluation and possibly pharmacological therapy are recommended.

faq.a: The Total/HDL ratio (also called cholesterol-HDL ratio) divides total cholesterol by HDL. The AHA recommends a value below 5.0, ideally below 3.5. Higher values indicate increased atherosclerosis risk.
LDL = Total Cholesterol − HDL − Triglycerides/5 (in mg/dL). This estimate is reliable when triglycerides are below 400 mg/dL. Above that threshold, a direct LDL measurement is required.

faq.q: What does the Triglyceride/HDL ratio mean?
faq.a: The Trig/HDL ratio is a marker for metabolic syndrome and insulin resistance. Values above 4 (mg/dL) are considered elevated. It correlates with small, dense LDL particles that are particularly atherogenic.

faq.q: What values should I aim for?
faq.a: Total/HDL: ideally < 3.5. LDL/HDL: < 2.5. Triglyceride/HDL: < 2. These targets apply to adults without known heart disease. Stricter targets apply with existing cardiovascular risk.

faq.q: How can I improve my ratios?
faq.a: Regular exercise, weight loss, and dietary changes (less saturated fat, more fiber, omega-3 fatty acids) measurably improve the lipid profile. Reducing alcohol effectively lowers triglycerides.

faq.q: How do I convert mg/dL to mmol/L?
faq.a: Cholesterol (Total, LDL, HDL): mg/dL ÷ 38.67 = mmol/L. Triglycerides: mg/dL ÷ 88.57 = mmol/L. The calculator handles this conversion automatically.
